title: Epacris pilosa
taxonomy: Epacris pilosa is classified within the plant family Epacridaceae, commonly known as the heath family. This family is part of the order Ericales, which includes other flowering plants like heaths and blueberries. Epacris pilosa belongs to the genus Epacris, native to Australia, New Zealand, and surrounding islands. It is a small shrub with distinctive red or pink flowers and is commonly known as the red or pink flowered Epacris. The classification of Epacris pilosa reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships within the plant kingdom.

intro: Epacris pilosa, commonly known as the hairy red flower or red alpine heath, is a small shrub native to the mountainous regions of Australia and New Zealand. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Epacris pilosa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from Epacris pilosa include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to aid in the relief of respiratory symptoms and to promote overall well-being.
